thesis: Deep investigation into FQHC Behavioral Health Director role across 6 research domains to validate market opportunity and define product requirements
description: Phase 1 research covering job profiles, FQHC operations, capacity planning, revenue cycle management, technology landscape, and compliance/regulatory requirements. Multi-domain investigation to understand operational realities, pain points, and vendor gaps.
verdict: Research complete - validated market opportunity with quantifiable pain points ($383K+ value per FQHC), clear vendor gaps (no platform solves FQHC billing + BH workflows), and actionable go-to-market strategy. Proceed to Phase 2 council debate for strategic decision (build vs buy vs partner).
key_findings: Directors manage 4-6 disconnected systems (EHR, scheduling, billing, compliance) with manual reconciliation taking 30+ minutes per operational question
15-25% claim denial rate (vs 5-10% medical/surgical) with 65% never resubmitted = $390K revenue leakage per FQHC annually
77% provider shortages, 93% burnout rate, 35-70% annual turnover = $562K cost per FQHC (documentation burden is #2 driver)
42 CFR Part 2 enforcement active Feb 16, 2026 (civil penalties up to $1.5M annually), FTCA deeming deadline June 26, 2026

No platform solves both FQHC billing (PPS, wraparound, sliding fee) AND BH workflows (treatment episodes, Part 2 consent, measurement-based care) - 60% of athenahealth FQHC users complain about weak BH modules
Market sizing: 1,400 FQHCs, 70%+ offer mental health (~1,000 target), $60-100M TAM, $4-20M ARR achievable in 3-5 years

federal_agencies: SAMHSA (Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration)
regulatory_documents: 42 CFR Part 2 Final Rule Fact Sheet

market_validation.target_segment: Mid-sized FQHCs (5-20 locations) with established BH programs

manual_reconciliation.platform_impact: Answer in <10 seconds with real-time validation

compliance_risk.scope_of_project_consequence: Billing recoupment, grant funding at risk

general_medical_ehrs.evidence: 60% of athenahealth FQHC users request better BH modules

bh_specific_platforms.evidence: Lack PPS encounter qualification logic, not FQHC-optimized

vendor_gap_analysis.market_gap: No vendor excels at both FQHC billing complexity AND behavioral health workflows comprehensively

strategic_options.recommended: Hybrid: Start with integration layer (athenahealth partnership), build standalone in parallel, migrate customers over 24-36 months

next_spoke.topic: Council debate - Build vs Buy vs Partner strategic decision
debate_topics: Build vs Buy vs Partner decision framework
Architecture approach (monolith vs microservices, event-driven validation)
Pricing model validation (base + performance vs alternatives)
Market entry strategy (pilot → scale → enterprise sequencing)
Compliance certification timeline (SOC 2, ONC, FTCA-approved vendor)
Technology stack (Node vs Python, AWS vs Azure)
Regulatory risk mitigation (externalize compliance logic, rapid deployment)
Partnership vs acquisition vs standalone positioning
next_spoke.output_format: Multi-perspective analysis with consensus recommendations + preserved dissenting opinions

research_confidence.rationale: High-quality primary sources (HRSA, CMS, SAMHSA official docs), multiple source triangulation for key statistics, direct citations to federal agencies and peer-reviewed studies. Lower confidence on capacity planning (fewer direct practitioner interviews, more inferred from adjacent research).

what_worked: Depth over breadth (80+ sources per domain vs superficial scan)
Quantification focus (every pain point has dollar value attached)
what_could_be_better: Earlier user interviews (direct practitioner validation, not just secondary research)
Competitive vendor demos (hands-on experience with athenahealth, NextGen, Qualifacts)
API quality assessment (actual integration testing before recommending integration layer)
Pricing validation (survey FQHCs on willingness-to-pay for performance-based model)
patterns_observed: Compliance > efficiency (Directors prioritize avoiding audit failures over productivity gains)
Integration pain is universal (4-6 systems is the norm, not exception)
Bolt-on modules fail (Legacy platforms retrofit BH onto medical, or FQHC billing onto BH - neither works well)
Performance-based pricing aligns incentives (Directors pay for outcomes, not features)
next_steps_for_validation: User interviews (5-10 Directors at pilot FQHCs)
Regulatory consultation (HRSA, state Medicaid on certification requirements)
